title: Savory Ukrainian Malai Crumble
description: A romantic, umami-packed crumble featuring tender beets, earthy mushrooms, and velvety malai, topped with a crumbly cheesebutter mixture-sugarfree and perfect for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: Grated cheese (e.g., farmer cheese or cheddar)

instructions: Wash, peel, and slice the beets into 1 cm rounds; blanch them in boiling water for 2 minutes, then simmer for 20 minutes until tender, draining well.

In a skillet, melt the butter over medium heat, add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and sliced mushrooms, cooking until the mushrooms are soft and lightly browned, about 8 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

Add the blanched beet slices to the skillet, stir to combine, and cook for an additional 2 minutes to let the flavors meld. Transfer the beet‑mushroom mixture to a 20 × 25 cm baking dish, spreading it evenly.

Whisk together the flour, grated cheese, remaining salt, and pepper in a small bowl until the mixture is uniformly dry. Fold in the melted butter using a pastry cutter or fork until the topping forms coarse, crumbly clumps.
Evenly sprinkle the crumble topping over the beet and mushroom layer in the dish. Spoon the sour cream (malai) over the crumble, using a spoon to swirl it into the crumbs for a mottled effect.
Bake uncovered for 20–25 minutes, or until the crumble is golden brown and the mixture is bubbling. Remove from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle chopped dill over the top for a fresh, herbal finish. Serve the crumble warm, allowing diners to scoop up the creamy, tangy layers and the crisp, buttery topping.
Enjoy this comforting, sugar‑free Ukrainian dessert‑style dish as a savory dinner accompaniment or a heart‑warming main course.
